concerns.text: The plan cites 'coref-data/davis_wsc_raw' and 'lshowway/wikipedia.VOS' as sources for video clips. These HuggingFace repositories appear to contain Parquet tables of metadata or text, not raw video files (MP4/AVI) required for optical flow computation and diffusion inference. The plan assumes these Parquet files contain the video data itself or direct links to it, but standard DAVIS/VOS datasets on HF are usually split into metadata and large binary blobs. If the Parquet files do not contain the actual video frames or valid, direct URLs to the video binaries, the pipeline cannot compute optical flow or run inference. The dataset citation is likely inappropriate for the modality (video) required.

The plan states: 'The specific LiveEdit weights will be loaded from the canonical HuggingFace model hub (if available) or the implementation will use the closest available pre-trained stable-diffusion checkpoint with temporal layers removed'. This is an inappropriate dataset/model assumption. The 'LiveEdit' paper relies on specific temporal attention layers and potentially specific fine-tuned weights. Replacing them with a generic 'closest available' SD checkpoint without the specific temporal architecture or weights invalidates the 'Baseline' comparison. The baseline must be the actual LiveEdit implementation (or a verified reproduction) to measure the trade-off of removing attention layers. Using a generic SD model as a baseline makes the 'memory reduction' claim meaningless as the architectures are fundamentally different.

The plan assumes running a diffusion-based video editing pipeline (even CPU-optimized) on 500 clips within 6 hours on a GitHub Actions free-tier (2 CPU, ~7GB RAM) is feasible. Diffusion inference is computationally intensive. Even with Farneback flow (CPU), the diffusion steps for 500 clips (likely 16-24 frames each) on a 2-core CPU runner will likely exceed the 6-hour limit by orders of magnitude. The dataset size (500 clips) is inappropriate for the compute constraints (GHA free-tier) and the modality (diffusion video). A smaller subset (e.g., 20-50 clips) or a different compute strategy is required.
